title: Cheirostylis moniliformis
intro: Cheirostylis moniliformis is a species of flowering plant in the family Diapensiaceae, commonly known as the "string-bead orchid" due to its unique, beaded flowers. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Cheirostylis moniliformis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which have been found to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory activities. In traditional medicine, Cheirostylis moniliformis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is used to treat conditions such as bronchitis, coughs, and sore throats.
taxonomy: Cheirostylis moniliformis is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. The genus Cheirostylis is a group of flowering plants endemic to tropical Asia, and C. moniliformis is one of the species found in this genus. This classification places C. moniliformis within the broader category of monocots, which are flowering plants with one cotyledon in their seeds. The specific classification of C. moniliformis reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.
